claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 10 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin. The first thing I need to know is about the child (or children) you're claiming for.

**How old is the child you want to claim Child Benefit for?**

Please let me know if they are:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children you'd like to claim for, please tell me each of their ages (and their names, so I can keep track).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Thanks for helping me with this. I'm wanting to claim Child Benefit for my two children - Alex, who's 10, and Blake, who's 2. So they're both under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! You're very welcome – I'm happy to help you work through this.

Great news that both Alex (10) and Blake (2) are under 16. Children under 16 automatically qualify in terms of age, so we can move on to the next check for both of them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
The next question is about whether the children are working:

**Are both Alex and Blake working less than 24 hours per week (or not working at all)?**

Given their ages (10 and 2), I'd expect the answer is yes – but I just need to confirm this to proceed.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh no, neither of them work at all! Alex is only 10 and Blake is just a toddler - only 2 years old. So yes, they're definitely not working.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's what I thought – just needed to confirm! With Alex at 10 and Blake at 2, they're certainly not working, so we can move forward.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are both Alex and Blake NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

This means they're not claiming benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit themselves. Given their ages, this would be very unusual – but I need to confirm that neither child is receiving such benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them receive any benefits in their own names. They're just children living at home with me - they don't claim anything themselves.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that makes sense. Let's continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are Alex and Blake in any kind of apprenticeship?**

Given their young ages (10 and 2), I'd assume the answer is no – but I need to confirm that neither child is currently doing an apprenticeship.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, definitely not! Alex is only 10 and Blake is just 2 - they're far too young for apprenticeships. Neither of them are doing anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly – far too young for apprenticeships! Let's move on to the next part of the assessment, which is about you (the claimant).

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status. You can qualify through **any one** of the following:

**Do any of these apply to you?**

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. **You have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **You have pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ continuous months)
4. **You have pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
5. **You have pre-settled status** with sufficient financial resources (and not claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit)
6. **You have pre-settled status** and are a student with sufficient resources
7. **You have pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with the right to reside
8. **You're a Crown servant** living abroad (or in certain qualifying countries)

The most common situation is simply that you normally live in the UK. Is that the case for you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, no - I don't live in the UK at the moment. I'm currently living abroad, and none of those other situations apply to me either. I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for being upfront about that. Unfortunately, this is important information for your eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry to share this news, but based on what you've told me, you would not currently be eligible for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Outcome: Not Eligible

I'm afraid you are **not currently eligible** for Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake.

### Summary

| Child | Age | Child Qualifies? | Eligible for Benefit? |
|-------|-----|------------------|----------------------|
| Alex | 10 | ✅ Yes | ❌ No |
| Blake | 2 | ✅ Yes | ❌ No |

### Reason
**You do not meet the UK residency requirements.**

While both Alex and Blake qualify as eligible children (they're under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in apprenticeships), Child Benefit requires the **claimant** to either:
- Normally live in the UK, **or**
- Meet specific alternative criteria (such as having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or being a Crown servant abroad)

Since you're currently living abroad and none of the alternative pathways apply to you, you cannot claim Child Benefit at this time.

### What You Can Do

- **If your circumstances change** – for example, if you return to live in the UK – you may become eligible and could apply at that point
- **If you think you might qualify** through one of the alternative routes (e.g., you obtain settled status, or become a Crown servant), you can reassess your eligibility
- **For more information**, visit: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility

I'm sorry I couldn't bring better news. Is there anything else you'd like me to clarify about Child Benefit eligibility?

reasonings: Alex (age 10) meets all the child-related criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements - they do not normally live in the UK and do not qualify through any of the alternative pathways (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, or Crown servant status). Therefore, the claimant cannot receive Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake (age 2) meets all the child-related criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements - they do not normally live in the UK and do not qualify through any of the alternative pathways (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, or Crown servant status). Therefore, the claimant cannot receive Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: Both children (Alex, 10, and Blake, 2) qualify as eligible children for Child Benefit purposes - they are under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in apprenticeships. However, the claimant does not meet the residency requirements for Child Benefit. They do not normally live in the UK and confirmed that none of the alternative qualifying pathways apply to them (such as settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or being a Crown servant). UK residency (or meeting specific alternative criteria) is a fundamental requirement for claiming Child Benefit, so the claim cannot proceed.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
